goal: Find every prim in the stage with PhysicsRigidBodyAPI applied using find_prims_by_schema — a single tool call, no manual traversal.
thoughts: The tool accepts either the applied-schema token ('PhysicsRigidBodyAPI') or the Python class name ('RigidBodyAPI') — it strips the conventional 'Physics' prefix and looks up in UsdPhysics. Don't drop to run_usd_script for what's a single-call registered tool.
failure_modes: Writing run_usd_script with prim.HasAPI(UsdPhysics.RigidBodyAPI) — works but bypasses the registered tool.
Passing 'RigidBodyAPI' only — also works (prefix-strip handles it), but the applied-schema token is the canonical input.
Looping PrimRange in a shell tool — wrong tier, find_prims_by_schema IS the purpose-built primitive.
qa_status: dialogue_canonical; structurally complete; not exercised in QA; classified 2026-05-15
